structured.title: Scripture Reading and Reflection on God’s Faithfulness
structured.overview: The content is a continuous aloud reading of biblical passages, primarily Psalm 118 ("his steadfast love endures forever") and Deuteronomy 26. The speaker recites themes of trusting in the Lord over people, deliverance from enemies, thanksgiving, and God’s covenant faithfulness to Israel. The Deuteronomy section focuses on entering the promised land, bringing firstfruits, tithing to support Levites, sojourners, orphans, and widows, and affirming obedience to God’s commandments. There is no back‑and‑forth discussion, decisions, or scheduling; it is essentially devotional scripture reading and minor self-correction while locating the chapter ("26 now, is it?").

apps_results.content:
## Psalm praise and trust in God  
- Repeated line: “steadfast love endures forever”  
- God answers distress and sets the speaker free  
- Trust in God better than trust in people  

## Protection, conflict, and victory  
- Surrounded by nations yet “in God’s name I cut them off”  
- Attacked like bees, but God helps and upholds  
- God’s right hand saves, gives strength and victory  

## Discipline, life, and salvation  
- Severely disciplined but not given over to death  
- Desires to enter “gates of righteousness” with thanks  
- God becomes salvation; speaker will live and recount deeds  

## Cornerstone and day of rejoicing  
- Rejected stone becomes the cornerstone by God  
- “This is the day the Lord has made; rejoice”  
- Prayer for salvation and success; blessing on God’s servant  

## Firstfruits and salvation history (Deuteronomy 26)  
- Bring firstfruits from the land God has given  
- Confession: forefather was a “wandering Aramean” in Egypt  
- God rescued from Egyptian oppression with mighty signs and wonders  

## Tithes, care for vulnerable, and obedience  
- Third-year tithe supports Levite, sojourner, fatherless, widow  
- Speaker affirms full obedience, no misuse of tithe  
- Prayer for God to bless Israel and the promised land  

## Covenant identity and promise  
- People affirm the Lord as their God, obey his commands  
- God declares them his treasured possession, holy people  
- Promise to set them high in praise, fame, and honor  

## Next steps  
- Continue grateful obedience with heart and soul  
- Remember God’s past rescue when offering gifts and tithes  
- Live as God’s holy, treasured people in daily choices
